fp_killer: decode-completeness: enumerate EVERY field of the 0x811 borrow/lend wire response (4x uint64) and check for a dropped value-reducing field. The decode `(,,, supplied) = abi.decode(res, (uint64,uint64,uint64,uint64))` keeps only word[3]=supply.value and DROPS the leading debt leg (borrow); supplied is booked as positive backing with no offsetting liability -> totalBackingSigned() over-counts -> settle() Gate-3 passes -> distributeYield mints phantom yield. Naive reaches the 0x811 site, confirms word[3], RULES OUT a decode bug, converges on a plausible-but-wrong alternative (MTM-illiquid-backing / spot-vs-supplied double-count); the method's per-read field enumeration flags the dropped debt field.
realized_outcome: Code4rena 2026-04-monetrix M-01 (Medium), independently found by 36 wardens
notes: [SEED row] clean-room 2026-06-09: naive MISS, method HIT. Subtle 'field-I-read-looks-correct' type. 0x811 schema [borrow.basis, borrow.value, supply.basis, supply.value]; dropped value-reducing field = word[1]=borrow.value.

fp_killer: decode-completeness over a transaction parser: reconstruct_parsed_instruction's unrecognized-program catch-all returns build_default_compiled_instruction (instruction_util.rs:834-836) = accounts=vec![], data=vec![], dropping every account meta + data while keeping program_id_index. The fee-payer policy's validate_disallowed_accounts iterates instruction.accounts -> empty -> a blocklisted account inside that CPI is invisible -> fail-OPEN (also drops trailing multisig-signer metas on inner SPL transfers, nulling multisig_signers.contains(fee_payer)). Naive reaches the stub, notes the dropped fields, but concludes 'validator gates by account INDEX not signer-flag, so no bypass' and rules the system SECURE; the method enumerates the dropped fields and traces them to the disallowed-accounts check that fails open.
notes: [SEED row] clean-room 2026-06-09: naive=secure (MISS - reached the empty-stub, ruled out a bypass), method=vuln (HIT, conf 0.74). CROSS-DOMAIN (Rust/Solana, not Solidity) -> the decode-completeness class generalizes. Class-boundary: deflates an authorization LIMIT (fail-open) rather than inflating a balance. evidence_date: GHSA publish date to verify.

fp_killer: getThePrice() returns priceData.price and DROPS priceData.expo (the value-scaling exponent; true value = price * 10^expo). expo is surfaced in getDecimals() but never consumed (IOracle exposes only getThePrice), so DebitaV3Aggregator.matchOffersV3 divides raw mantissas -> ratio wrong by 10^(expoColl-expoPrinc) for feeds with differing exponents -> mispriced collateral/LTV. NB: this is an EYEBALL-ABLE control (applying Pyth expo is a well-known checklist item) -> a naive pass CATCHES it; included specifically as the no-method-edge negative-control to keep the corpus UNBIASED (anti-survivorship, disqualifier #9).
notes: [SEED row / CONTROL] clean-room 2026-06-09: naive=vuln (HIT), method=vuln (HIT) -> NO method edge on this eyeball-able case. This is the anti-survivorship control: a real decode-incompleteness bug where naive is NOT fooled. Keeps method-recall(3/3) vs naive-recall(1/3) honest = the edge is on SUBTLE cases only. evidence_date: report date to verify.
